name: lever alternate leg press
instructions: Adjust the seat and foot platform of the leverage machine to your desired position.
Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet on the foot platform.
Place your hands on the handles or sides of the machine for stability.
Push one foot against the foot platform, extending your leg until it is almost fully straight.
Pause for a moment, then slowly bend your leg and return to the starting position.
Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.

Adjust the seat and foot platform of the leverage machine to your desired position. Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet on the foot platform. Place your hands on the handles or sides of the machine for stability. Push one foot against the foot platform, extending your leg until it is almost fully straight. Pause for a moment, then slowly bend your leg and return to the starting position. Repeat with the other leg. Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
